title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI on AWS and Slackbot's AI Leap
summary: OpenAI models hit AWS, Slackbot gets AI boost

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Today, we're looking at some interesting developments in the AI space. OpenAI's frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS. This is a big deal because it gives enterprises a new way to work with OpenAI's powerful tools using the AWS environment they're already familiar with. It's like adding a new tool to your favorite Swiss Army knife – it's just more useful now.

OpenAI's models, including their cutting-edge frontier models and Codex, are now generally available on AWS. This move is significant because it allows businesses to leverage OpenAI's AI capabilities within the AWS ecosystem, making it easier to integrate and manage. It's a win-win for both OpenAI and AWS users. For one, it expands OpenAI's reach and makes its tools more accessible. For AWS users, it means they can tap into OpenAI's advanced AI features without having to switch platforms.

In other news, Salesforce has rolled out a new version of Slackbot, its workplace assistant. This isn't your old Slackbot; it's been transformed into a fully powered AI agent. Imagine having a super-smart sidekick that can actually help you get work done, rather than just sending notifications. That's what Slackbot is aiming for, and it's clear Salesforce is pushing hard to stay competitive in the workplace AI space. With Microsoft and Google also vying for dominance, it's exciting to see how these tech giants will innovate and improve their offerings.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
